title: SSRF: actor_cache fetches keyId URI without SSRF guard
description:
actor_cache::get_public_key() in src/actor_cache.rs directly fetches the actor_uri parameter with no SSRF guard. This URI is derived from the attacker-controlled keyId field of the Signature header (server.rs line 461: key_id.split('#').next()). An attacker can POST to /inbox or /users/{username}/inbox with Signature: keyId="http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/",...  and the server will fetch that URL on the attacker's behalf before rejecting the signature.

All other SSRF-sensitive fetches in the codebase are guarded (follower_actor, inbox_uri, shared_inbox_uri, media URLs). This one was missed.

Fix: add is_private_host() check in actor_cache::get_public_key() before the HTTP fetch, rejecting non-https URIs and private/internal hosts. The same guard already applied to follower_actor in server.rs lines 538-548 should be replicated here.
close_reason: SSRF guard added to actor_cache::get_public_key: https-only + is_private_host check
